segments.title: CEPA Explained: Canada's Flagship Environmental Law
segments.prompt_template: Write a deep-dive segment explaining the Canadian Environmental Protection Act (CEPA). Cover its history, the 2023 amendments recognizing the right to a healthy environment, how CEPA regulates toxic substances, the priority substances list process, and what practitioners need to know about compliance obligations.

segments.title: Carbon Pricing Mechanisms in Canada
segments.prompt_template: Write a deep-dive segment explaining Canada's carbon pricing system. Cover the federal carbon levy, the output-based pricing system for large emitters, provincial systems (BC carbon tax, Quebec cap-and-trade), how carbon credits work, and the ongoing political debate about the consumer carbon tax.

segments.title: PFAS: The Forever Chemical Challenge
segments.prompt_template: Write a deep-dive segment about PFAS contamination and remediation in Canada. Cover what PFAS are, why they are called 'forever chemicals,' major contamination sites in Canada, emerging remediation technologies (incineration, electrochemical oxidation, filtration), and the evolving regulatory landscape for PFAS limits in drinking water.

segments.title: How Federal Impact Assessments Actually Work
segments.prompt_template: Write a deep-dive segment explaining the federal impact assessment process under the Impact Assessment Act. Cover the planning phase, the actual assessment, the decision statement, Indigenous consultation requirements, and how the Supreme Court reference decision in 2023 changed the scope of federal jurisdiction.

segments.title: Brownfield Redevelopment in Canadian Cities
segments.prompt_template: Write a deep-dive segment about brownfield site redevelopment in Canada. Cover the environmental assessment process, provincial contaminated sites registries, risk-based closure standards versus generic standards, financial incentives for redevelopment, and successful case studies of former industrial sites converted to community use.

segments.title: Profile: Environment and Climate Change Canada
segments.prompt_template: Write a profile segment about Environment and Climate Change Canada (ECCC) as an institution. Cover its mandate, key divisions (enforcement, science, policy), how it interacts with provincial regulators, notable enforcement actions, and how its role has evolved with climate change becoming a top policy priority.

segments.title: Profile: Indigenous Guardian Programs
segments.prompt_template: Write a profile segment about Indigenous Guardian programs in Canada. Cover how these programs integrate traditional ecological knowledge with modern environmental monitoring, notable programs across the country, their role in impact assessments and species protection, and how they are reshaping environmental governance.

segments.title: The Great Lakes Cleanup: A 50-Year Success Story
segments.prompt_template: Write a historical perspective segment about the cleanup of the Great Lakes from the 1970s to today. Cover the burning Cuyahoga River, the Canada-US Great Lakes Water Quality Agreement, the Areas of Concern program, phosphorus reduction successes, and remaining challenges like microplastics and invasive species.

segments.title: The Evolution of Canadian Environmental Law
segments.prompt_template: Write a historical perspective segment tracing the evolution of Canadian environmental law from the 1970s to today. Cover the creation of Environment Canada, the first Clean Air Act, CEPA 1988 and 1999, the Species at Risk Act, the Impact Assessment Act, and the key court decisions that shaped environmental jurisdiction.

segments.title: Nuclear Energy for Canada: Friend or Foe?
segments.prompt_template: Write a balanced contrarian segment debating nuclear energy's role in Canada's clean energy future. Cover the pro-nuclear arguments (reliable baseload, low carbon, SMR potential, existing CANDU expertise) and the anti-nuclear arguments (waste storage, cost overruns, construction timelines, Indigenous concerns near proposed sites). Present both sides fairly.

segments.title: The Carbon Capture Debate
segments.prompt_template: Write a contrarian segment examining the debate around carbon capture and storage (CCS) in Canada. Cover the bull case (necessary for heavy industry, supported by tax credits, Alberta's Quest project success) and the bear case (expensive distraction, moral hazard for fossil fuels, limited proven capacity). Reference Canadian projects specifically.

segments.title: How to Read an Environmental Site Assessment
segments.prompt_template: Write a practical utility segment teaching listeners how to read a Phase I and Phase II Environmental Site Assessment (ESA). Cover the purpose of each phase, key sections to focus on, how to interpret contaminant concentrations against provincial standards, what 'areas of potential environmental concern' mean, and red flags to watch for.

segments.title: Environmental Compliance Checklist for SMEs
segments.prompt_template: Write a practical utility segment providing an environmental compliance checklist for small and medium enterprises in Canada. Cover air emissions reporting, wastewater discharge permits, hazardous waste storage and transport, spill reporting obligations, and the most common compliance gaps that trigger enforcement actions.

segments.title: Canada's Methane Regulations: Oil and Gas Sector
segments.prompt_template: Write a deep-dive segment about Canada's methane emissions regulations for the oil and gas sector. Cover the federal methane reduction targets, the proposed 75% reduction by 2030, leak detection and repair requirements, provincial equivalency agreements, and how Canada's approach compares to US EPA methane rules.

segments.title: PFAS: The Forever Chemicals Crisis
segments.prompt_template: Write a deep-dive about PFAS contamination. Cover what PFAS are (per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances), why they're called 'forever chemicals', major contamination sites across Canada, Health Canada's evolving drinking water guidelines, US EPA CERCLA designation, remediation technologies (granular activated carbon, ion exchange, PFAS destruction), and the regulatory trajectory for PFAS restrictions in Canada and internationally.

segments.title: Carbon Pricing in Canada: How It Actually Works
segments.prompt_template: Write a deep-dive explaining Canada's carbon pricing system. Cover the federal backstop mechanism, provincial systems (BC's carbon tax, Quebec's cap-and-trade, Alberta's TIER system), the Canada Carbon Rebate, how large emitters are treated differently from consumers, the legal challenges (Supreme Court reference), and how Canada's approach compares to the EU ETS and other international carbon markets.

segments.title: Mine Site Remediation: Cleaning Up Canada's Legacy
segments.prompt_template: Write a deep-dive about mine site remediation in Canada. Cover the Federal Contaminated Sites Action Plan, iconic sites like Giant Mine (arsenic trioxide) in Yellowknife, Faro Mine in Yukon, the acid mine drainage problem, passive vs active treatment systems, financial assurance requirements for new mines, and the estimated $10+ billion liability for abandoned mines across Canada.

segments.title: Environmental Impact Assessment: The Canadian Process
segments.prompt_template: Write a deep-dive about environmental impact assessment in Canada. Cover the Impact Assessment Act (IAA), the 2023 Supreme Court reference decision that found parts unconstitutional, the federal-provincial coordination challenges, Indigenous consultation requirements (duty to consult and accommodate), the role of the Impact Assessment Agency of Canada, and how the process compares to NEPA in the US and EU EIA directives.

segments.title: Brownfield Redevelopment: Turning Contamination into Opportunity
segments.prompt_template: Write a deep-dive about brownfield redevelopment in Canada. Cover provincial contaminated sites registries (BC Site Registry, Ontario Environmental Site Registry), risk-based vs prescriptive cleanup standards, the role of qualified professionals, vapour intrusion assessment requirements, institutional controls and risk management measures, insurance products for environmental liability, and successful case studies of industrial sites transformed into mixed-use developments.

segments.title: Drinking Water Quality: Standards and Challenges
segments.prompt_template: Write a deep-dive about drinking water quality regulation in Canada. Cover the Guidelines for Canadian Drinking Water Quality, how they differ from enforceable US EPA MCLs, provincial implementation variability, First Nations drinking water advisories (the ongoing crisis), emerging contaminants (microplastics, pharmaceuticals, PFAS), source water protection, and the Infrastructure gap facing small and rural systems.

segments.title: Profile: Environment and Climate Change Canada
segments.prompt_template: Write a profile of Environment and Climate Change Canada (ECCC). Cover its mandate spanning pollution prevention, wildlife protection, weather forecasting, and climate policy. Discuss key legislation it administers (CEPA, Species at Risk Act, Fisheries Act pollution provisions), the enforcement branch, the Canadian Environmental Protection Act review, and how the department balances environmental protection with economic development pressures.

segments.title: Profile: Ecojustice and Environmental Law
segments.prompt_template: Write a profile of Ecojustice, Canada's largest environmental law charity. Cover landmark cases they've litigated (Species at Risk Act enforcement, coal plant challenges, plastic pollution), their strategic litigation approach, how environmental law differs from environmental regulation, the role of citizen suits and standing requirements in Canadian environmental law, and why legal advocacy is a critical complement to regulatory enforcement.

segments.title: The Walkerton Water Crisis: Lessons Learned
segments.prompt_template: Write a historical segment about the Walkerton E. coli water contamination crisis of 2000. Cover what happened (7 deaths, 2,300 ill), the root causes identified by the O'Connor Inquiry (undertrained operators, provincial deregulation, inadequate monitoring), the regulatory reforms that followed (Ontario Clean Water Act, source water protection), and how Walkerton transformed drinking water governance across Canada. Connect to ongoing vulnerabilities.

segments.title: The Sydney Tar Ponds: Canada's Largest Contaminated Site
segments.prompt_template: Write a historical segment about the Sydney Tar Ponds in Nova Scotia. Cover the century of steel mill and coke oven operation, the discovery of massive PAH and heavy metal contamination, the decades-long cleanup debate, the $400M remediation using solidification/stabilization technology completed in 2013, community health impacts, and lessons learned about industrial legacy contamination and environmental justice in Canada.

segments.title: Contrarian: Can Canada Actually Hit Net Zero by 2050?
segments.prompt_template: Write a balanced contrarian segment examining whether Canada's net zero by 2050 target is achievable. The optimistic case: declining renewable costs, carbon capture technology, electric vehicle adoption, hydrogen potential. The skeptical case: oil sands emissions trajectory, infrastructure timelines, political will across provinces, the gap between pledges and policies, and the math on what 'net zero' actually requires in terms of annual emissions reductions. Present both sides with specific Canadian data.

segments.title: Contrarian: Is Recycling Actually Working?
segments.prompt_template: Write a balanced contrarian segment examining whether recycling programs deliver on their environmental promises. Cover contamination rates in curbside programs, the economics of recycling vs virgin materials, what happens to Canadian recyclables after China's National Sword policy, extended producer responsibility schemes (BC's Recycle BC model), the circular economy vision vs reality, and whether reducing consumption matters more than recycling. Use Canadian data.

segments.title: How to Read a Contaminated Sites Report
segments.prompt_template: Write a practical utility segment teaching environmental professionals how to efficiently read a Phase I or Phase II Environmental Site Assessment. Cover the ASTM E1527/CSA Z768 standard structure, the most important sections to focus on (recognized environmental conditions, data gaps, recommendations), how to evaluate analytical results against applicable standards, common red flags in reports, and what questions to ask the assessor. Practical, actionable guidance.

segments.title: Navigating Environmental Permits in Canada
segments.prompt_template: Write a practical utility segment about environmental permitting in Canada. Cover the major permit types (effluent discharge, air emissions, waste management, water withdrawal), the federal vs provincial jurisdiction split, the typical timeline and costs, common reasons applications get delayed or rejected, Indigenous consultation requirements, and tips for preparing a strong application. Focus on the practical realities that professionals encounter.

segments.title: Listener Guide: Careers in Environmental Science
segments.prompt_template: Write a practical listener guide about careers in Canadian environmental science and consulting. Cover the main career paths (consulting, government, industry, NGO, academia), required qualifications and professional designations (P.Eng, P.Geo, EP, QP), salary ranges, what a typical day looks like for a contaminated sites consultant, emerging specializations (climate adaptation, PFAS, ESG), and advice for students and career changers. Be honest about both the rewards and challenges.

segments.title: Listener Guide: Environmental Spill Response
segments.prompt_template: Write a practical listener guide about environmental spill response obligations in Canada. Cover federal and provincial reporting thresholds, the first 30 minutes (containment, notification, documentation), required notifications (Transport Canada for rail, provincial ministry, municipality), the role of the responsible party vs contractors, common mistakes that increase liability, and how to prepare a site-specific spill response plan. Practical, step-by-step guidance.
